response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced polyglot engineer with a long career spanning web, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems, but his resume shows no meaningful alignment with the core demands of this Principal/IC6 role. The position centers specifically on experimentation platforms, telemetry infrastructure, A/B testing frameworks, feature flighting, and growth measurement systems — none of which appear anywhere in his work history. His current Staff-level title and the complete absence of evidence for statistical analysis, experiment assignment/targeting, or cross-team growth architecture leadership make him a poor fit for a Principal-level growth engineering role.
strengths: Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, C, C++, Java, D) covering most of the required language stack
Direct experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency constraints
Recent AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work at re:cinq demonstrates continued technical relevance and hands-on depth
concerns: Zero evidence of experimentation platform design, A/B testing, feature flagging, rollout automation, or bias detection
No mention of telemetry infrastructure, metrics pipelines, or statistical/data analysis capabilities anywhere in the resume
No demonstrated Principal-level technical leadership or track record driving cross-team growth strategy and measurement systems

key_factors.factor: No experimentation or growth platform experience
No telemetry, metrics, or statistical analysis background
